title: Ghost Stories - Taoist Monks, Hopping Vampires, and Brides with White Hair
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RO6gVW9tdbY

A scripted review of a stone-cold classic: Ghost Stories, the notoriously brutal cooperative tower-defence game of Taoist monks holding a village against an endless tide of Chinese ghosts and demons. Daniel (@dungeondive) frames it through the lens he loves best — the martial-arts horror cinema of Mr. Vampire, A Chinese Ghost Story, Spooky Encounters, and The Boxer's Omen — and opens with a generous watchlist for anyone wanting to explore the films behind the game, from Kung Fu Zombie to Holy Flame of the Martial World. His verdict is that Ghost Stories is a rare case of perfect marriage between theme and mechanism: the frantic, overwhelmed feel of those films — a handful of heroes defending a villa against supernatural onslaught — translated directly into a game where every turn must accomplish something and a single mistake can see the village haunted into defeat. Buckets with holes, buckets without handles, buckets of cursed water that spread the fire. Daniel has owned the game since 2008 and has won only a small handful of times, yet the losses never feel wasted — they feel like training for the next match, which he argues only deepens the martial-arts theme.

----

Ghost Stories punishes a single wasted turn, and Daniel loves it for that. Where's your line on difficulty in co-ops — does a near-unwinnable game feel like training, or just cruelty?
